title: eryngium amethystinum var. transiens
intro: Eryngium amethystinum var. transiens, commonly known as the purple sea lavender, is a flowering plant in the carrot family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of purple sea lavender include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
